reasoning_chain_text:
1. For each school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22, identify Washington counties with low-income enrollment between 32 and 40 percent.
2. Intersect county sets across all four years.
3. For 2021-22 assessment data, compute district-average math proficiency by county for counties in the intersection.
4. Keep counties with average proficiency above 40 percent.
5. Return county names in alphabetical order, separated by comma and space.
original_reasoning_chain: Node 1: 2018-19 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Whitman}
Node 2: 2019-20 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Whitman}
Node 3: 2020-21 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Thurston, Whitman}
Node 4: 2021-22 Low-Income 32-40% -> {King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman}
HOP 2 (d=1, data.gov filter - node 5 depends on hop 1):
Node 5: 2021-22 Math proficiency >40% -> King, Kitsap, Whitman
original_ifnal_question: Among Washington counties with low-income student enrollment between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22, which had district-average Math proficiency above 40% in 2021-22? List the county names in alphabetical order separated by `, `. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
